code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test16-AUG-2023Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Markedly low HbA1c with normal serum glucose may indicate recent hypoglycemia or anemia. Findings are consistent with possible chronic kidney disease and renal glycosuria; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
